summary: Completed backend API testing for N51ZW Flight Tracker. All 14 pytest tests passed (100%). Validated: root tracker info (icao24=a6616a, registration=N51ZW), GET /api/aircraft/state schema and required fields, events/history endpoints return lists, settings GET/PUT with persistence, settings clamping (5→10 lower bound; 10000→600 upper bound), poll-now triggers OpenSky integration successfully, all 4 test-event types (takeoff/landing/signal_lost/signal_available) create events and appear in events list, invalid event type returns 400. Live OpenSky integration verified: auth_mode='basic' (basic auth with provided credentials accepted), last_poll_success=true after poll-now. Aircraft not transmitting ADS-B (no state vector returned), which is expected for a WWII P51D Mustang not currently in flight — endpoints handle this gracefully without crashing.

minor.issue: Backend clamps poll_interval_seconds to max 600 (10 minutes), but PRD/problem statement says configurable up to 5 minutes (300s). The 600s cap conflicts with the stated 10s-5min range. Either the UI range should be 10s-10min, or the server max should be 300. The test expectation (clamp to 600) matches current code; flagging spec inconsistency.

action_items: Reconcile poll_interval upper bound: problem statement says 5min (300s) max but server clamps at 600s. Decide canonical max and align server + frontend UI.
Optional: add a real OpenSky integration assertion path once aircraft transmits — currently last_snapshot stays null because P51D is not airborne.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~528 lines — still under the 700 line threshold but approaching it. Consider splitting OpenSkyClient, state-machine (classify/process_poll), and routers into separate modules for maintainability.

OpenSkyClient.fetch_state has no rate-limit backoff beyond logging; with a 10s minimum poll interval, anonymous users will hit OpenSky rate limits quickly. Consider exponential backoff or honoring X-Rate-Limit-Retry-After header.
process_poll re-queries last snapshot on every tick; could cache previous_state in memory to reduce DB load.
emit_event after a 'signal_lost' synthetic marker does not update last_poll_at/auth_mode coherently if subsequent calls fail repeatedly.
CORSMiddleware is added AFTER include_router (works in Starlette but conventional order is before). Cosmetic only.
test_credentials: OpenSky basic auth (Warnherr-P51) configured in backend/.env — verified working (auth_mode=basic returned).
seed_data_creation: Test events (takeoff, landing, signal_lost, signal_available) were created via /api/aircraft/test-event/* during testing and persisted to MongoDB events_coll. Settings document is the singleton row in flight_settings (restored to poll_interval_seconds=300, sound_enabled=true after test mutations).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid. All endpoints under /api work. OpenSky basic auth credentials are accepted. Aircraft N51ZW is not transmitting ADS-B right now so last_snapshot is null in /api/aircraft/state — this is expected. Test events from prior runs persist in MongoDB (4 test events were created). If frontend testing is requested next, use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://n51zw-live.preview.emergentagent.com. Note inconsistency between problem statement (5min max) and server clamp (600s max).
